verses.bsb: If the clouds are full, they will pour out rain upon the earth; whether a tree falls to the south or to the north, in the place where it falls, there it will lie.

verses.esv: He who observes the wind will not sow, and he who regards the clouds will not reap.
verses.kjv: He that observeth the wind shall not sow; and he that regardeth the clouds shall not reap.
verses.bsb: He who watches the wind will fail to sow, and he who observes the clouds will fail to reap.

verses.esv: As you do not know the way the spirit comes to the bones in the womb of a woman with child, so you do not know the work of God who makes everything.
verses.kjv: As thou knowest not what is the way of the spirit, nor how the bones do grow in the womb of her that is with child: even so thou knowest not the works of God who maketh all.
verses.bsb: As you do not know the path of the wind, or how the bones are formed in a mother’s womb, so you cannot understand the work of God, the Maker of all things.

verses.esv: In the morning sow your seed, and at evening withhold not your hand, for you do not know which will prosper, this or that, or whether both alike will be good.
verses.kjv: In the morning sow thy seed, and in the evening withhold not thine hand: for thou knowest not whether shall prosper, either this or that, or whether they both shall be alike good.
verses.bsb: Sow your seed in the morning, and do not rest your hands in the evening, for you do not know which will succeed, whether this or that, or if both will equally prosper.

verses.esv: Light is sweet, and it is pleasant for the eyes to see the sun.
verses.kjv: ¶ Truly the light is sweet, and a pleasant thing it is for the eyes to behold the sun:
verses.bsb: Light is sweet, and it pleases the eyes to see the sun.

verses.esv: So if a person lives many years, let him rejoice in them all; but let him remember that the days of darkness will be many. All that comes is vanity.
verses.kjv: But if a man live many years, and rejoice in them all; yet let him remember the days of darkness; for they shall be many. All that cometh is vanity.
verses.bsb: So if a man lives many years, let him rejoice in them all. But let him remember the days of darkness, for they will be many. Everything to come is futile.

verses.esv: Rejoice, O young man, in your youth, and let your heart cheer you in the days of your youth. Walk in the ways of your heart and the sight of your eyes. But know that for all these things God will bring you into judgment.
verses.kjv: ¶ Rejoice, O young man, in thy youth; and let thy heart cheer thee in the days of thy youth, and walk in the ways of thine heart, and in the sight of thine eyes: but know thou, that for all these things God will bring thee into judgment.
verses.bsb: Rejoice, O young man, while you are young, and let your heart be glad in the days of your youth. Walk in the ways of your heart and in the sight of your eyes, but know that for all these things God will bring you to judgment.

verses.esv: Remove vexation from your heart, and put away pain from your body, for youth and the dawn of life are vanity.
verses.kjv: Therefore remove sorrow from thy heart, and put away evil from thy flesh: for childhood and youth are vanity.
verses.bsb: So banish sorrow from your heart, and cast off pain from your body, for youth and vigor are fleeting.

verses.esv: Remember also your Creator in the days of your youth, before the evil days come and the years draw near of which you will say, “I have no pleasure in them”;
verses.kjv: REMEMBER now thy Creator in the days of thy youth, while the evil days come not, nor the years draw nigh, when thou shalt say, I have no pleasure in them;
verses.bsb: Remember your Creator in the days of your youth, before the days of adversity come and the years approach of which you will say, “I find no pleasure in them,”

verses.esv: before the sun and the light and the moon and the stars are darkened and the clouds return after the rain,
verses.kjv: While the sun, or the light, or the moon, or the stars, be not darkened, nor the clouds return after the rain:
verses.bsb: before the light of the sun, moon, and stars is darkened, and the clouds return after the rain,

verses.esv: in the day when the keepers of the house tremble, and the strong men are bent, and the grinders cease because they are few, and those who look through the windows are dimmed,
verses.kjv: In the day when the keepers of the house shall tremble, and the strong men shall bow themselves, and the grinders cease because they are few, and those that look out of the windows be darkened,
verses.bsb: on the day the keepers of the house tremble and the strong men stoop, when those grinding cease because they are few and those watching through windows see dimly,

verses.esv: and the doors on the street are shut—when the sound of the grinding is low, and one rises up at the sound of a bird, and all the daughters of song are brought low—
verses.kjv: And the doors shall be shut in the streets, when the sound of the grinding is low, and he shall rise up at the voice of the bird, and all the daughters of musick shall be brought low;
verses.bsb: when the doors to the street are shut and the sound of the mill fades away, when one rises at the sound of a bird and all the daughters of song grow faint,

verses.esv: they are afraid also of what is high, and terrors are in the way; the almond tree blossoms, the grasshopper drags itself along, and desire fails, because man is going to his eternal home, and the mourners go about the streets—
verses.kjv: Also when they shall be afraid of that which is high, and fears shall be in the way, and the almond tree shall flourish, and the grasshopper shall be a burden, and desire shall fail: because man goeth to his long home, and the mourners go about the streets:
verses.bsb: when men fear the heights and dangers of the road, when the almond tree blossoms, the grasshopper loses its spring, and the caper berry shrivels— for then man goes to his eternal home and mourners walk the streets.

verses.esv: before the silver cord is snapped, or the golden bowl is broken, or the pitcher is shattered at the fountain, or the wheel broken at the cistern,
verses.kjv: Or ever the silver cord be loosed, or the golden bowl be broken, or the pitcher be broken at the fountain, or the wheel broken at the cistern.
verses.bsb: Remember Him before the silver cord is snapped and the golden bowl is crushed, before the pitcher is shattered at the spring and the wheel is broken at the well,

verses.esv: and the dust returns to the earth as it was, and the spirit returns to God who gave it.
verses.kjv: Then shall the dust return to the earth as it was: and the spirit shall return unto God who gave it.
verses.bsb: before the dust returns to the ground from which it came and the spirit returns to God who gave it.

verses.esv: Vanity of vanities, says the Preacher; all is vanity.
verses.kjv: ¶ Vanity of vanities, saith the preacher; all is vanity.
verses.bsb: “Futility of futilities,” says the Teacher. “Everything is futile!”

verses.esv: Besides being wise, the Preacher also taught the people knowledge, weighing and studying and arranging many proverbs with great care.
verses.kjv: And moreover, because the preacher was wise, he still taught the people knowledge; yea, he gave good heed, and sought out, and set in order many proverbs.
verses.bsb: Not only was the Teacher wise, but he also taught the people knowledge; he pondered, searched out, and arranged many proverbs.

verses.esv: The Preacher sought to find words of delight, and uprightly he wrote words of truth.
verses.kjv: The preacher sought to find out acceptable words: and that which was written was upright, even words of truth.
verses.bsb: The Teacher searched to find delightful sayings and to record accurate words of truth.

verses.esv: The words of the wise are like goads, and like nails firmly fixed are the collected sayings; they are given by one Shepherd.
verses.kjv: The words of the wise are as goads, and as nails fastened by the masters of assemblies, which are given from one shepherd.
verses.bsb: The words of the wise are like goads, and the anthologies of the masters are like firmly embedded nails driven by a single Shepherd.

verses.esv: My son, beware of anything beyond these. Of making many books there is no end, and much study is a weariness of the flesh.
verses.kjv: And further, by these, my son, be admonished: of making many books there is no end; and much study is a weariness of the flesh.
verses.bsb: And by these, my son, be further warned: There is no end to the making of many books, and much study wearies the body.

verses.esv: The end of the matter; all has been heard. Fear God and keep his commandments, for this is the whole duty of man.
verses.kjv: ¶ Let us hear the conclusion of the whole matter: Fear God, and keep his commandments: for this is the whole duty of man.
verses.bsb: When all has been heard, the conclusion of the matter is this: Fear God and keep His commandments, because this is the whole duty of man.

verses.esv: For God will bring every deed into judgment, with every secret thing, whether good or evil.
verses.kjv: For God shall bring every work into judgment, with every secret thing, whether it be good, or whether it be evil. THE
verses.bsb: For God will bring every deed into judgment, along with every hidden thing, whether good or evil.
